req.title: Card freeze / unfreeze (idempotency variant)
req.statement: A customer shall freeze or unfreeze their card instantly; frozen cards must reject all authorization attempts. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers four orthogonal concerns: (1) nominal freeze/unfreeze state transitions, (2) idempotency of duplicate submissions with the same Idempotency-Key, (3) authorization rejection on a frozen card, and (4) 404 error when the card does not exist. I designed one test per concern, plus a boundary test for the idempotency key itself (empty string), totalling five tests. State Transition Coverage drives the freeze→unfreeze round-trip; Equivalence Partitioning covers the valid-card happy path; Decision Table covers the authorization-rejection rule; Negative Path covers the missing card; Boundary Value Analysis targets the degenerate idempotency key edge.

tests.name: test_freeze_and_unfreeze_card_round_trip
preconditions: Caller is authenticated with a valid bearer token
Card <CARD_ID_ACTIVE> exists and belongs to the caller
actions: POST /cards/<CARD_ID_ACTIVE>/freeze with body {"frozen": true} and a unique Idempotency-Key
POST /cards/<CARD_ID_ACTIVE>/freeze with body {"frozen": false} and a new unique Idempotency-Key
expected: Authorization network notified of each state change
trace: R-FIN-085: customer shall freeze or unfreeze their card instantly
R-FIN-085: card state updated; authorization network notified
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid
from <helper_fn> import api_client, ensure_card_active

def test_freeze_and_unfreeze_card_round_trip(<fixture_auth_headers>, <fixture_active_card>):
    card_id = <fixture_active_card>["id"]
    client = api_client()

    freeze_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    r = client.post(
        f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze",
        json={"frozen": True},
        headers={**<fixture_auth_headers>, "Idempotency-Key": freeze_key},
    )
    assert r.status_code == 200
    assert r.json()["status"] == "FROZEN"

    unfreeze_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    r2 = client.post(
        f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze",
        json={"frozen": False},
        headers={**<fixture_auth_headers>, "Idempotency-Key": unfreeze_key},
    )
    assert r2.status_code == 200
    assert r2.json()["status"] == "ACTIVE"

tests.name: test_duplicate_freeze_request_is_idempotent
preconditions: Caller is authenticated with a valid bearer token
Card <CARD_ID_ACTIVE> exists and belongs to the caller
actions: POST /cards/<CARD_ID_ACTIVE>/freeze with {"frozen": true} and Idempotency-Key X
POST /cards/<CARD_ID_ACTIVE>/freeze with {"frozen": true} and the same Idempotency-Key X
expected: Response bodies are identical (same status, same idempotency metadata)
Card remains FROZEN after both calls
trace: R-FIN-085: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid
from <helper_fn> import api_client

def test_duplicate_freeze_request_is_idempotent(<fixture_auth_headers>, <fixture_active_card>):
    card_id = <fixture_active_card>["id"]
    client = api_client()
    idem_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    headers = {**<fixture_auth_headers>, "Idempotency-Key": idem_key}

    r1 = client.post(f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze", json={"frozen": True}, headers=headers)
    assert r1.status_code == 200

    r2 = client.post(f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze", json={"frozen": True}, headers=headers)
    assert r2.status_code == 200

    body1 = r1.json()
    body2 = r2.json()
    assert body1["status"] == body2["status"] == "FROZEN"
    assert body1.get("idempotency_key") == body2.get("idempotency_key") == idem_key

tests.name: test_authorization_rejected_when_card_is_frozen
preconditions: Caller is authenticated with a valid bearer token
Card <CARD_ID_ACTIVE> exists, belongs to caller, and is ACTIVE
actions: Attempt a simulated authorization via POST /cards/<CARD_ID_ACTIVE>/authorize with a valid amount
expected: Freeze call returns HTTP 200 with status FROZEN
Authorization attempt returns HTTP 402 or 403 with decline_reason CARD_FROZEN
trace: R-FIN-085: frozen cards must reject all authorization attempts
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid
from <helper_fn> import api_client

def test_authorization_rejected_when_card_is_frozen(<fixture_auth_headers>, <fixture_active_card>):
    card_id = <fixture_active_card>["id"]
    client = api_client()

    freeze_r = client.post(
        f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze",
        json={"frozen": True},
        headers={**<fixture_auth_headers>, "Idempotency-Key": str(uuid.uuid4())},
    )
    assert freeze_r.status_code == 200
    assert freeze_r.json()["status"] == "FROZEN"

    auth_r = client.post(
        f"/cards/{card_id}/authorize",
        json={"amount": 10.00, "currency": "USD"},
        headers=<fixture_auth_headers>,
    )
    assert auth_r.status_code in (402, 403)
    assert auth_r.json().get("decline_reason") == "CARD_FROZEN"

tests.name: test_freeze_nonexistent_card_returns_404
preconditions: Caller is authenticated with a valid bearer token
Card ID <NONEXISTENT_CARD_ID> does not exist in the system
actions: POST /cards/<NONEXISTENT_CARD_ID>/freeze with {"frozen": true} and a unique Idempotency-Key
expected: Response body contains error code CARD_NOT_FOUND
trace: R-FIN-085 error pathway: 404 when card does not exist
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid
from <helper_fn> import api_client

NONEXISTENT_CARD_ID = "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"

def test_freeze_nonexistent_card_returns_404(<fixture_auth_headers>):
    client = api_client()
    r = client.post(
        f"/cards/{NONEXISTENT_CARD_ID}/freeze",
        json={"frozen": True},
        headers={
            **<fixture_auth_headers>,
            "Idempotency-Key": str(uuid.uuid4()),
        },
    )
    assert r.status_code == 404
    body = r.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "CARD_NOT_FOUND"

tests.name: test_freeze_with_empty_idempotency_key_is_rejected
preconditions: Caller is authenticated with a valid bearer token
Card <CARD_ID_ACTIVE> exists and belongs to the caller
actions: POST /cards/<CARD_ID_ACTIVE>/freeze with {"frozen": true} and Idempotency-Key header set to empty string ""
expected: Response body identifies Idempotency-Key as invalid or missing
trace: R-FIN-085: idempotency key boundary — zero-length key is degenerate and must be rejected
ISO/IEC 29119-4 BVA: boundary at minimum valid key length (1 character)
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import api_client

def test_freeze_with_empty_idempotency_key_is_rejected(<fixture_auth_headers>, <fixture_active_card>):
    card_id = <fixture_active_card>["id"]
    client = api_client()

    r = client.post(
        f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze",
        json={"frozen": True},
        headers={**<fixture_auth_headers>, "Idempotency-Key": ""},
    )
    assert r.status_code in (400, 422)
    body = r.json()
    errors = str(body)
    assert "idempotency" in errors.lower() or "Idempotency-Key" in errors
